notes: Core retail in-store transaction processing; price management and inventory feeding back-office.
Used for supply chain communication between retailers and suppliers.
Decision support for retail; querying promotions impact, product popularity, consumer profiles.
Vertical-specific ERP integration gaining traction in retail; SAP America cited.
Majority of new retail IS now client-server; migration from mainframes and proprietary minis.
Significant retail IT managers choosing NT for client-server apps despite Unix reliability reputation.
Reliable and durable; many existing retail systems; losing ground to NT for new deployments.
Proprietary minicomputer platform being maintained alongside newer architectures.
Emerging as critical new retail channel; also used for supply chain communication.
Tighter retailer-supplier integration reducing excess inventory and duplicate processes.
Improving merchandising selection, reducing inventory, increasing gross margins.
Reconstructing retail payment infrastructure; new pan-European currency (euro) connection.
Adoption cited as major IT trend for retail environments (in-store and supply chain).
